chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Research: Best-Selling Programming Books of All Time
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for conducting and compiling research to inform the IVD
# book's structure, style, and positioning. Intent artifact precedes research.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system), ivd_system_intent.yaml
# Version: 1.1
# Created: 2026-01-25
# Status: Active
# ==============================================================================

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "research"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this research accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Research best-selling programming books of all time to extract numbers, patterns, structural and writing-style insights, and author backstories for IVD book strategy."
  
  goal: |
    Produce a single canonical research document that:
    1. NUMBERS: Sales figures, rankings, longevity, editions, print runs, and comparable metrics from official or trusted industry sources.
    2. PATTERNS: What makes these books sell—themes, timing, audience, format, publisher, and recurring success factors.
    3. STRUCTURE: How top sellers organize content—parts, chapters, front/back matter, pedagogy (theory-first, project-based, reference), pacing, and length.
    4. WRITING STYLE: Voice, tone, use of narrative vs. reference, code-to-prose ratio, examples, clarity tactics, and distinct stylistic signatures.
    5. AUTHOR BACKGROUND: Full backstory for each author—origin, career path, how they came to write the book, expertise, and public reception.
    
    All claims must be traceable to official or trusted sources. No unsourced assertions.
  
  success_metric: |
    - One canonical research doc (no summary or temp files)
    - Each of the 5 dimensions (numbers, patterns, structure, style, authors) has a dedicated section with substantive content
    - Every factual claim has an inline or referenced source (publisher, author site, industry list, reputable journalism, academic or trade publication)
    - At least 8–12 distinct books covered in depth; additional titles in comparative tables where useful
    - Cross-validation against this intent: all deliverables present, gaps documented and addressed
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (structure, style, positioning decisions)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (evidence for rationale and constraints)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections in the research output
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  section_1_numbers:
    title: "Numbers: Sales, Rankings, Longevity"
    required_content:
      - "Ranked or ordered list of best-selling programming/CS books with figures where available"
      - "Sources: publisher statements, Nielsen/BookScan, industry lists (e.g. IEEE, O'Reilly, Amazon categori

, positioning decisions)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (evidence for rationale and constraints)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections in the research output
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  section_1_numbers:
    title: "Numbers: Sales, Rankings, Longevity"
    required_content:
      - "Ranked or ordered list of best-selling programming/CS books with figures where available"
      - "Sources: publisher statements, Nielsen/BookScan, industry lists (e.g. IEEE, O'Reilly, Amazon categories), author/publisher sites"
      - "Metrics: copies sold, editions, years in print, peak rankings, awards"
      - "Explicit 'source' or 'attribution' for each figure; 'estimated' or 'reported' when not official"
  
  section_2_patterns:
    title: "Patterns: What Makes Them Sell"
    required_content:
      - "Recurring themes (readability, timelessness, first-mover, pedagogy, brand)"
      - "Timing and market (era, technology wave, gap in market)"
      - "Audience (beginner, professional, academic, reference)"
      - "Format (paperback, hardcover, ebook; single vs. series)"
      - "Publisher and distribution (O'Reilly, Addison-Wesley, Manning, self-pub, etc.)"
      - "Synthesized patterns with examples from the books studied"
  
  section_3_structure:
    title: "Structure: How Top Sellers Organize Content"
    required_content:
      - "Part/chapter patterns (how many parts, chapters per part, length)"
      - "Front matter (preface, foreword, how-to-use, TOC style)"
      - "Back matter (appendices, glossary, index, references)"
      - "Pedagogical approaches: theory-first, project-based, reference, hybrid"
      - "Pacing and progression (spiral, linear, modular)"
      - "Comparative table or structured summary across books"
  
  section_4_writing_style:
    title: "Writing Style and Tone"
    required_content:
      - "Voice: authoritative, conversational, mentor-like, formal, playful"
      - "Tone: technical depth vs. accessibility, humor, seriousness"
      - "Code-to-prose ratio and role of examples (central vs. supportive)"
      - "Clarity tactics: analogies, diagrams, repetition, summaries, exercises"
      - "Distinct stylistic signatures (e.g. 'Clean Code' brevity, 'Pragmatic' wit)"
      - "Quotes or short extracts where they illustrate style (with citation)"
  
  section_5_author_background:
    title: "Author Background: Full Backstories"
    required_content:
      - "For each main author: origin, education, career before the book"
      - "How they came to write the book (trigger, motivation, opportunity)"
      - "Expertise and credibility (projects, employers, community role)"
      - "Public reception (awards, reviews, influence on industry)"
      - "Sources: author bios, interviews, publisher pages, Wikipedia (with caveat), reputable profiles"
  
  section_6_synthesis:
    title: "Synthesis and Implications for the IVD Book"
    required_content:
      - "Summary of findings most relevant to IVD book (structure, style, positioning)"
      - "Author experience and case studies: impl

- "For each main author: origin, education, career before the book"
      - "How they came to write the book (trigger, motivation, opportunity)"
      - "Expertise and credibility (projects, employers, community role)"
      - "Public reception (awards, reviews, influence on industry)"
      - "Sources: author bios, interviews, publisher pages, Wikipedia (with caveat), reputable profiles"
  
  section_6_synthesis:
    title: "Synthesis and Implications for the IVD Book"
    required_content:
      - "Summary of findings most relevant to IVD book (structure, style, positioning)"
      - "Author experience and case studies: implication that case studies and examples should stem from the author's direct experience (anonymized) or be explicitly labeled as composite; pattern from Section 5 (Fowler, McConnell, GoF, Hunt & Thomas: from practice, consulting, design work)"
      - "Explicit gaps in this research and how they were addressed or acknowledged"
      - "Cross-validation: checklist against this intent’s deliverables"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "official_or_trusted_sources"
    requirement: "All factual claims must cite official or trusted sources (publisher, author, industry list, IEEE/ACM, reputable tech or literary press)"
    test: "Manual: every number, quote, and biographical claim has an inline or end reference"
    consequence_if_violated: "Research cannot be used as evidence for IVD book decisions; credibility at risk"
  
  - name: "no_temp_or_summary"
    requirement: "Single canonical research document only; no TEMP, SUMMARY, or RELEASE files in research/"
    test: "find book/research -name '*TEMP*' -o -name '*SUMMARY*' -o -name '*RELEASE*' | wc -l == 0"
    consequence_if_violated: "Violates IVD structural_cleanliness; package integrity"
  
  - name: "all_deliverables_present"
    requirement: "All 6 deliverable sections (numbers, patterns, structure, style, authors, synthesis) must exist and contain substantive content"
    test: "Manual: section headers present; each has at least 3–5 substantive subsections or equivalent content"
    consequence_if_violated: "Intent not satisfied; research incomplete for IVD book strategy"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Conduct structured research on best-selling programming books before locking IVD book structure and style."
  
  reasoning: |
    - IVD book aims for market impact (Amazon category, sales, adoption). Evidence from proven sellers reduces guesswork.
    - Structure and writing style are high-leverage; replicating patterns of success is informed by data, not taste alone.
    - Author backstories provide positioning and narrative models (expertise, origin story, credibility).
    - One canonical doc keeps research/ clean; no summary or temp docs (IVD structural_cleanliness).
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (market_driven, evid

duct structured research on best-selling programming books before locking IVD book structure and style."
  
  reasoning: |
    - IVD book aims for market impact (Amazon category, sales, adoption). Evidence from proven sellers reduces guesswork.
    - Structure and writing style are high-leverage; replicating patterns of success is informed by data, not taste alone.
    - Author backstories provide positioning and narrative models (expertise, origin story, credibility).
    - One canonical doc keeps research/ clean; no summary or temp docs (IVD structural_cleanliness).
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (market_driven, evidence_based constraints)"
  date: "2026-01-25"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Focus only on recent or AI-related tech books"
    rejected_because: "IVD book should learn from 'all-time' bestsellers; longevity and timelessness are patterns we need. AI-only is too narrow."
    when_it_works: "If the goal were only to position against current AI books."
  
  - name: "Multiple smaller research docs per dimension"
    rejected_because: "Increases fragmentation; IVD favors one canonical artifact per research scope. Easier to cross-validate and maintain."
    when_it_works: "Very large research programs with different owners per dimension."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Sales and ranking data are often proprietary or estimated"
    action: "Use best-available trusted sources; label 'reported', 'estimated', or 'industry consensus' where not official"
    monitor: "Each number in section 1 has an attribution"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Author backstories may rely on Wikipedia or informal bios"
    action: "Prefer author sites, publisher bios, and interviews; note when Wikipedia or less formal source is used"
    monitor: "Section 5 citations"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  current: "research/best_selling_programming_books.md"
  intent_artifact: "research/best_selling_programming_books_intent.yaml"
  version: 1
  sources_to_consult:
    - "Publishers: O'Reilly, Addison-Wesley, Pearson, Manning, No Starch, Pragmatic Bookshelf"
    - "Industry: IEEE, ACM, Stack Overflow / developer surveys, Amazon category and ranking data"
    - "Authors: official bios, personal sites, interviews (e.g. Software Engineering Radio, podcasts)"
    - "Press: IEEE Software, Communications of the ACM, Wired, Ars Technica, publisher blogs and press releases"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-validation with this intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cr

- "Publishers: O'Reilly, Addison-Wesley, Pearson, Manning, No Starch, Pragmatic Bookshelf"
    - "Industry: IEEE, ACM, Stack Overflow / developer surveys, Amazon category and ranking data"
    - "Authors: official bios, personal sites, interviews (e.g. Software Engineering Radio, podcasts)"
    - "Press: IEEE Software, Communications of the ACM, Wired, Ars Technica, publisher blogs and press releases"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-validation with this intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_validation_checklist:
    - check: "Section 1 (Numbers) exists with sales/rankings and attributions"
      validates: "deliverables.section_1_numbers"
    - check: "Section 2 (Patterns) exists with themes, timing, audience, format, publisher"
      validates: "deliverables.section_2_patterns"
    - check: "Section 3 (Structure) exists with part/chapter, front/back matter, pedagogy, pacing"
      validates: "deliverables.section_3_structure"
    - check: "Section 4 (Writing style) exists with voice, tone, code-prose, clarity, signatures"
      validates: "deliverables.section_4_writing_style"
    - check: "Section 5 (Author background) exists with backstory for each main author"
      validates: "deliverables.section_5_author_background"
    - check: "Section 6 (Synthesis) exists with implications and gap acknowledgment"
      validates: "deliverables.section_6_synthesis"
    - check: "No TEMP/SUMMARY/RELEASE files in research/"
      validates: "constraints.no_temp_or_summary"
    - check: "Factual claims have sources"
      validates: "constraints.official_or_trusted_sources"
  
  gap_handling: "If a deliverable cannot be fully satisfied (e.g. no official sales data for a title), document the gap in Section 6 and note 'addressed by: [explanation]'."

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "section_6_synthesis required_content: add 'Author experience and case studies' so the synthesis must state that case studies/examples should stem from author's experience or be labeled composite. Aligns research intent with research doc 6.1 and book/chapter intents."
    reason: "Gap: finding was implied by Section 5, not originally required in 6; book Ch1 used composite. Now explicit."
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "Initial intent artifact; research to follow"
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation"

"1.0"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "Initial intent artifact; research to follow"
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation"
